action.hypothesis: The routed soft-codebook two-stage family has already shown strong gains at K=16, 64, 96, and 128, and its earlier K=32 result was slightly better than the current listed K=32 frontier anchor. A clean confirmatory rerun at K=32 under the pinned winning recipe is the highest-value next step because it can add a more valuable smaller-K non-dominated point while avoiding another policy-rejected code edit.
action.summary: Run one parameter-only confirmation experiment at K=32 with `routed_lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual`, keeping `EXPANSION_FACTOR=12`, `OPTIMIZER=signum`, `LR=3.2e-3`, and `AUXK_ALPHA=0.0` fixed. This directly tests whether the routed family should also own the K=32 frontier slot under the same recipe that already wins at K=16 and the higher-K anchors.
action.self_review: This stays within the fixed execution layer and uses only allowed env overrides after the previous code-edit round was policy-rejected. The hypothesis is narrow and high-value: verify whether routing improves the important mid-K anchor at K=32 under an otherwise frozen recipe. The main risk is that the prior K=32 routed win was noise and the plain two-stage soft-codebook model remains slightly better there.
notes_to_memory: Round 98's code-edit hypothesis was policy-rejected, so round 99 should return to env-only experiments.
The most valuable unresolved frontier question is whether the routed soft-codebook family also owns the K=32 EF=12 slot under the pinned winning recipe.
A K=32 routed confirmation would extend the routed family farther down the Pareto frontier and strengthen it as the main EF=12 backbone.
next_hypotheses: If routed wins at K=32, run the same fixed recipe at K=24 to test whether the routed advantage extends one more step into the smaller-K regime.
If routed is very close but does not win at K=32, keep plain `lowrank_two_stage_soft_codebook_residual` at K=32 and shift follow-up effort to K=24 or K=64 cleanup instead of more K=32 repeats.
If routed clearly loses at K=32, conclude that the routed advantage starts above this band and keep the family focused on K>=64 plus the established K=16 slot.

This directly tests whether the routed family should also own the K=32 frontier slot under the same recipe that already wins at K=16 and the higher-K anchors.
